items.headline: Shane van Gisbergen will defend NASCAR win at Watkins Glen from the pole position
items.description:
WATKINS GLEN, N.Y. (AP) — Shane van Gisbergen celebrated his 37th birthday on Saturday by gifting himself the top starting spot at Watkins Glen International.
The Trackhouse Racing driver qualified first on the 2.45-mile road course, earning the fifth pole of his NASCAR Cup Series career in his 62nd start — the fastest to reach that mark in nearly 20 years ( Denny Hamlin needed 49 starts to capture his fifth pole in April 2007).

items.headline: Rays-Red Sox game postponed by rain, rescheduled as part of doubleheader on July 17
items.description:
BOSTON (AP) — Saturday's game between the Tampa Bay Rays and Boston Red Sox was postponed by rain about 15 minutes before it was scheduled to start.
It will be made up as part of a day-night doubleheader on July 17.

items.headline: Udinese back English striker Davis over 'disgraceful' racial abuse
items.description: Italian Serie A club Udinese said on Saturday it was fully supporting striker Keinan Davis who claimed he was subjected to racist abuse during...

items.headline: John Harbaugh considers his first Giants rookie minicamp a rehearsal more than a competition
items.description:
EAST RUTHERFORD, N.J. (AP) — With the NFL season and his first game that counts as New York Giants coach more than four months away, John Harbaugh is taking things slowly.
The second and final rookie minicamp session Saturday lacked physicality by design. Harbaugh wants top-10 draft picks Arvell Reese and Francis "“Sisi” Mauigoa and the rest of the players involved to understand it's not a competitive camp but rather a chance to prepare for the next phase of offseason workouts.

items.headline: Jeeno Thitikul holds off every contender to stay in the lead at Mizuho Americas Open
items.description:
WEST CALDWELL, N.J. (AP) — Jeeno Thitikul held off every challenge Saturday at the Mizuho Americas Open and made nothing worse than par over her last 16 holes for a 2-under 70 and a two-shot lead as the Thai golfer goes after her first LPGA title of the year.
Thitikul started with a three-shot lead and it was gone quickly after a bogey on the second hole and a fast start by Hannah Green.
